NKJV.1: He who is often rebuked, and hardens his neck, Will suddenly be destroyed, and that without remedy.
NKJV.2: When the righteous are in authority, the people rejoice; But when a wicked man rules, the people groan.
NKJV.3: Whoever loves wisdom makes his father rejoice, But a companion of harlots wastes his wealth.
NKJV.4: The king establishes the land by justice, But he who receives bribes overthrows it.
NKJV.5: A man who flatters his neighbor Spreads a net for his feet.
NKJV.6: By transgression an evil man is snared, But the righteous sings and rejoices.
NKJV.7: The righteous considers the cause of the poor, But the wicked does not understand such knowledge.
NKJV.8: Scoffers set a city aflame, But wise men turn away wrath.
NKJV.9: If a wise man contends with a foolish man, Whether the fool rages or laughs, there is no peace.
NKJV.10: The bloodthirsty hate the blameless, But the upright seek his well-being.
NKJV.11: A fool vents all his feelings, But a wise man holds them back.
NKJV.12: If a ruler pays attention to lies, All his servants become wicked.
NKJV.13: The poor man and the oppressor have this in common: The LORD gives light to the eyes of both.
NKJV.14: The king who judges the poor with truth, His throne will be established forever.
NKJV.15: The rod and rebuke give wisdom, But a child left to himself brings shame to his mother.
NKJV.16: When the wicked are multiplied, transgression increases; But the righteous will see their fall.
NKJV.17: Correct your son, and he will give you rest; Yes, he will give delight to your soul.
NKJV.18: Where there is no revelation, the people cast off restraint; But happy is he who keeps the law.
NKJV.19: A servant will not be corrected by mere words; For though he understands, he will not respond.
NKJV.20: Do you see a man hasty in his words? There is more hope for a fool than for him.
NKJV.21: He who pampers his servant from childhood Will have him as a son in the end.
NKJV.22: An angry man stirs up strife, And a furious man abounds in transgression.
NKJV.23: A man’s pride will bring him low, But the humble in spirit will retain honor.
NKJV.24: Whoever is a partner with a thief hates his own life;  He swears to tell the truth, but reveals nothing.
NKJV.25: The fear of man brings a snare, But whoever trusts in the LORD shall be safe.
NKJV.26: Many seek the ruler’s favor, But justice for man comes from the LORD.
NKJV.27: An unjust man is an abomination to the righteous, And he who is upright in the way is an abomination to the wicked.

KJV.9: If a wise<S>2450</S> man<S>376</S> contendeth<S>8199</S> with a foolish<S>191</S> man<S>376</S>, whether he rage<S>7264</S> or laugh<S>7832</S>, there is no rest<S>5183</S>.
KJV.10: The bloodthirsty<S>582</S> <S>1818</S> hate<S>8130</S> the upright<S>8535</S>: but the just<S>3477</S> seek<S>1245</S> his soul<S>5315</S>. <sup>The bloodthirsty: Heb. Men of blood</sup>
KJV.11: A fool<S>3684</S> uttereth<S>3318</S> all his mind<S>7307</S>: but a wise<S>2450</S> man keepeth<S>7623</S> it in till afterwards<S>268</S>.

KJV.13: The poor<S>7326</S> and the deceitful<S>8501</S> man<S>376</S> meet together<S>6298</S>: the LORD<S>3068</S> lighteneth<S>215</S> both<S>8147</S> their eyes<S>5869</S>. <sup>the deceitful: or, the usurer</sup>
KJV.14: The king<S>4428</S> that faithfully<S>571</S> judgeth<S>8199</S> the poor<S>1800</S>, his throne<S>3678</S> shall be established<S>3559</S> for ever<S>5703</S>.
KJV.15: The rod<S>7626</S> and reproof<S>8433</S> give<S>5414</S> wisdom<S>2451</S>: but a child<S>5288</S> left<S>7971</S> to himself bringeth his mother<S>517</S> to shame<S>954</S>.

KJV.22: An angry<S>639</S> man<S>376</S> stirreth up<S>1624</S> strife<S>4066</S>, and a furious<S>2534</S> man<S>1167</S> aboundeth in<S>7227</S> transgression<S>6588</S>.

KJV.26: Many<S>7227</S> seek<S>1245</S> the ruler's<S>4910</S> favour<S>6440</S>; but every man's<S>376</S> judgment<S>4941</S> cometh from the LORD<S>3068</S>. <sup>the ruler's: Heb. the face of a ruler</sup>
KJV.27: An unjust<S>5766</S> man<S>376</S> is an abomination<S>8441</S> to the just<S>6662</S>: and he that is upright<S>3477</S> in the way<S>1870</S> is abomination<S>8441</S> to the wicked<S>7563</S>.

ESV.1: He who is often reproved, yet stiffens his neck, will suddenly be broken beyond healing.
ESV.2: When the righteous increase, the people rejoice, but when the wicked rule, the people groan.
ESV.3: He who loves wisdom makes his father glad, but a companion of prostitutes squanders his wealth.
ESV.4: By justice a king builds up the land, but he who exacts gifts  tears it down.
ESV.5: A man who flatters his neighbor spreads a net for his feet.
ESV.6: An evil man is ensnared in his transgression, but a righteous man sings and rejoices.
ESV.7: A righteous man knows the rights of the poor; a wicked man does not understand such knowledge.
ESV.8: Scoffers set a city aflame, but the wise turn away wrath.
ESV.9: If a wise man has an argument with a fool, the fool only rages and laughs, and there is no quiet.
ESV.10: Bloodthirsty men hate one who is blameless and seek the life of the upright.
ESV.11: A fool gives full vent to his spirit, but a wise man quietly holds it back.
ESV.12: If a ruler listens to falsehood, all his officials will be wicked.
ESV.13: The poor man and the oppressor meet together; the LORD gives light to the eyes of both.
ESV.14: If a king faithfully judges the poor, his throne will be established forever.
ESV.15: The rod and reproof give wisdom, but a child left to himself brings shame to his mother.
ESV.16: When the wicked increase, transgression increases, but the righteous will look upon their downfall.
ESV.17: Discipline your son, and he will give you rest; he will give delight to your heart.
ESV.18: Where there is no prophetic vision the people cast off restraint,  but blessed is he who keeps the law.
ESV.19: By mere words a servant is not disciplined, for though he understands, he will not respond.
ESV.20: Do you see a man who is hasty in his words? There is more hope for a fool than for him.
ESV.21: Whoever pampers his servant from childhood will in the end find him his heir.
ESV.22: A man of wrath stirs up strife, and one given to anger causes much transgression.
ESV.23: One's pride will bring him low, but he who is lowly in spirit will obtain honor.
ESV.24: The partner of a thief hates his own life; he hears the curse, but discloses nothing.
ESV.25: The fear of man lays a snare, but whoever trusts in the LORD is safe.
ESV.26: Many seek the face of a ruler, but it is from the LORD that a man gets justice.
ESV.27: An unjust man is an abomination to the righteous, but one whose way is straight is an abomination to the wicked.

NASB.1: A man who hardens [his] neck after much reproof Will suddenly be broken beyond remedy.
NASB.2: When the righteous increase, the people rejoice, But when a wicked man rules, people groan.
NASB.3: A man who loves wisdom makes his father glad, But he who keeps company with harlots wastes [his] wealth.
NASB.4: The king gives stability to the land by justice, But a man who takes bribes overthrows it.
NASB.5: A man who flatters his neighbor Is spreading a net for his steps.
NASB.6: By transgression an evil man is ensnared, But the righteous sings and rejoices.
NASB.7: The righteous is concerned for the rights of the poor, The wicked does not understand [such] concern.
NASB.8: Scorners set a city aflame, But wise men turn away anger.
NASB.9: When a wise man has a controversy with a foolish man, The foolish man either rages or laughs, and there is no rest.
NASB.10: Men of bloodshed hate the blameless, But the upright are concerned for his life.
NASB.11: A fool always loses his temper, But a wise man holds it back.
NASB.12: If a ruler pays attention to falsehood, All his ministers [become] wicked.
NASB.13: The poor man and the oppressor have this in common: The LORD gives light to the eyes of both.
NASB.14: If a king judges the poor with truth, His throne will be established forever.
NASB.15: The rod and reproof give wisdom, But a child who gets his own way brings shame to his mother.
NASB.16: When the wicked increase, transgression increases; But the righteous will see their fall.
NASB.17: Correct your son, and he will give you comfort; He will also delight your soul.
NASB.18: Where there is no vision, the people are unrestrained, But happy is he who keeps the law.
NASB.19: A slave will not be instructed by words [alone]; For though he understands, there will be no response.
NASB.20: Do you see a man who is hasty in his words? There is more hope for a fool than for him.
NASB.21: He who pampers his slave from childhood Will in the end find him to be a son.
NASB.22: An angry man stirs up strife, And a hot-tempered man abounds in transgression.
NASB.23: A man's pride will bring him low, But a humble spirit will obtain honor.
NASB.24: He who is a partner with a thief hates his own life; He hears the oath but tells nothing.
NASB.25: The fear of man brings a snare, But he who trusts in the LORD will be exalted.
NASB.26: Many seek the ruler's favor, But justice for man [comes] from the LORD.
NASB.27: An unjust man is abominable to the righteous, And he who is upright in the way is abominable to the wicked.

NLT.1:
Whoever stubbornly refuses to accept criticism
will suddenly be destroyed beyond recovery.
NLT.2:
When the godly are in authority, the people rejoice.
But when the wicked are in power, they groan.
NLT.3:
The man who loves wisdom brings joy to his father,
but if he hangs around with prostitutes, his wealth is wasted.
NLT.4:
A just king gives stability to his nation,
but one who demands bribes destroys it.
NLT.5:
To flatter friends
is to lay a trap for their feet.
NLT.6:
Evil people are trapped by sin,
but the righteous escape, shouting for joy.
NLT.7:
The godly care about the rights of the poor;
the wicked don’t care at all.
NLT.8:
Mockers can get a whole town agitated,
but the wise will calm anger.
NLT.9:
If a wise person takes a fool to court,
there will be ranting and ridicule but no satisfaction.
NLT.10:
The bloodthirsty hate blameless people,
but the upright seek to help them.
NLT.11:
Fools vent their anger,
but the wise quietly hold it back.
NLT.12:
If a ruler pays attention to liars,
all his advisers will be wicked.
NLT.13:
The poor and the oppressor have this in common —
the LORD gives sight to the eyes of both.
NLT.14:
If a king judges the poor fairly,
his throne will last forever.
NLT.15:
To discipline a child produces wisdom,
but a mother is disgraced by an undisciplined child.
NLT.16:
When the wicked are in authority, sin flourishes,
but the godly will live to see their downfall.
NLT.17:
Discipline your children, and they will give you peace of mind
and will make your heart glad.
NLT.18:
When people do not accept divine guidance, they run wild.
But whoever obeys the law is joyful.
NLT.19:
Words alone will not discipline a servant;
the words may be understood, but they are not heeded.
NLT.20:
There is more hope for a fool
than for someone who speaks without thinking.
NLT.21:
A servant pampered from childhood
will become a rebel.
NLT.22:
An angry person starts fights;
a hot-tempered person commits all kinds of sin.
NLT.23:
Pride ends in humiliation,
while humility brings honor.
NLT.24:
If you assist a thief, you only hurt yourself.
You are sworn to tell the truth, but you dare not testify.
NLT.25:
Fearing people is a dangerous trap,
but trusting the LORD means safety.
NLT.26:
Many seek the ruler’s favor,
but justice comes from the LORD.
NLT.27:
The righteous despise the unjust;
the wicked despise the godly.

WEB.1: He who is often rebuked and stiffens his neck will be destroyed suddenly, with no remedy.
WEB.2: When the righteous thrive, the people rejoice; but when the wicked rule, the people groan.
WEB.3: Whoever loves wisdom brings joy to his father; but a companion of prostitutes squanders his wealth.
WEB.4: The king by justice makes the land stable, but he who takes bribes tears it down.
WEB.5: A man who flatters his neighbor spreads a net for his feet.
WEB.6: An evil man is snared by his sin, but the righteous can sing and be glad.
WEB.7: The righteous care about justice for the poor. The wicked aren’t concerned about knowledge.
WEB.8: Mockers stir up a city, but wise men turn away anger.
WEB.9: If a wise man goes to court with a foolish man, the fool rages or scoffs, and there is no peace.
WEB.10: The bloodthirsty hate a man of integrity; and they seek the life of the upright.
WEB.11: A fool vents all of his anger, but a wise man brings himself under control.
WEB.12: If a ruler listens to lies, all of his officials are wicked.
WEB.13: The poor man and the oppressor have this in common: Yahweh gives sight to the eyes of both.
WEB.14: The king who fairly judges the poor, his throne shall be established forever.
WEB.15: The rod of correction gives wisdom, but a child left to himself causes shame to his mother.
WEB.16: When the wicked increase, sin increases; but the righteous will see their downfall.
WEB.17: Correct your son, and he will give you peace; yes, he will bring delight to your soul.
WEB.18: Where there is no revelation, the people cast off restraint; but one who keeps the law is blessed.
WEB.19: A servant can’t be corrected by words. Though he understands, yet he will not respond.
WEB.20: Do you see a man who is hasty in his words? There is more hope for a fool than for him.
WEB.21: He who pampers his servant from youth will have him become a son in the end.
WEB.22: An angry man stirs up strife, and a wrathful man abounds in sin.
WEB.23: A man’s pride brings him low, but one of lowly spirit gains honor.
WEB.24: Whoever is an accomplice of a thief is an enemy of his own soul. He takes an oath, but dares not testify.
WEB.25: The fear of man proves to be a snare, but whoever puts his trust in Yahweh is kept safe.
WEB.26: Many seek the ruler’s favor, but a man’s justice comes from Yahweh.
WEB.27: A dishonest man detests the righteous, and the upright in their ways detest the wicked.

CSB17.1:
One who becomes stiff-necked,
after many reprimands
will be shattered instantly —
beyond recovery. ⓛ
CSB17.2:
When the righteous flourish, the people rejoice,
but when the wicked rule, people groan. ⓜ
CSB17.3:
A man who loves wisdom brings joy to his father, ⓝ
but one who consorts with prostitutes destroys his wealth. ⓐ
CSB17.4:
By justice a king brings stability to a land, ⓑ
but a person who demands “contributions” [1]
demolishes it. ⓒ
CSB17.5:
A person who flatters [2] his neighbor
spreads a net for his feet. ⓓ
CSB17.6:
An evil person is caught by sin, ⓔ
but the righteous one sings and rejoices. ⓕ
CSB17.7:
The righteous person knows the rights [3] of the poor, ⓖ
but the wicked one does not understand these concerns.
CSB17.8:
Mockers inflame a city, ⓗ
but the wise turn away anger.
CSB17.9:
If a wise person goes to court with a fool,
there will be ranting and raving but no resolution. [4] ⓘ
CSB17.10:
Bloodthirsty men hate an honest person, ⓙ
but the upright care about him. [5]
CSB17.11:
A fool gives full vent to his anger, [6] ⓚ
but a wise person holds it in check. ⓛ
CSB17.12:
If a ruler listens to lies, ⓜ
all his officials will be wicked.
CSB17.13:
The poor and the oppressor have this in common: [7]
the LORD gives light to the eyes of both. ⓝ
CSB17.14:
A king who judges the poor with fairness —
his throne will be established forever. ⓐ
CSB17.15:
A rod of correction imparts wisdom, ⓑ
but a youth left to himself [8]
is a disgrace to his mother. ⓒ
CSB17.16:
When the wicked increase, rebellion increases,
but the righteous will see their downfall. ⓓ
CSB17.17:
Discipline your child, ⓔ and it will bring you peace of mind
and give you delight.
CSB17.18:
Without revelation [9] ⓕ people run wild, ⓖ
but one who follows divine instruction will be happy. ⓗ
CSB17.19:
A servant cannot be disciplined by words;
though he understands, he doesn’t respond. ⓘ
CSB17.20:
Do you see someone who speaks too soon? ⓙ
There is more hope for a fool than for him. ⓚ
CSB17.21:
A servant pampered from his youth
will become arrogant [10] later on.
CSB17.22:
An angry person stirs up conflict,
and a hot-tempered one [11] increases rebellion. ⓛ
CSB17.23:
A person’s pride will humble him, ⓜ
but a humble spirit will gain honor. ⓝ
CSB17.24:
To be a thief’s partner is to hate oneself;
he hears the curse but will not testify. ⓐ
CSB17.25:
The fear of mankind is a snare, ⓑ
but the one who trusts in the LORD is protected. [12] ⓒ
CSB17.26:
Many desire a ruler’s favor, ⓓ
but a person receives justice from the LORD. ⓔ
CSB17.27:
An unjust person is detestable to the righteous,
and one whose way is upright
is detestable to the wicked. ⓕ

AMP.1:
He who hardens his neck and refuses instruction after being often reproved (corrected, criticized),
Will suddenly be broken beyond repair.
AMP.2:
When the righteous are in authority and become great, the people rejoice;
But when the wicked man rules, the people groan and sigh.
AMP.3:
A man who loves [skillful and godly] wisdom makes his father joyful,
But he who associates with prostitutes wastes his wealth.
AMP.4:
The king establishes (stabilizes) the land by justice,
But a man who takes bribes overthrows it.
AMP.5:
A man who flatters his neighbor [with smooth words intending to do harm]
Is spreading a net for his own feet.
AMP.6:
By his wicked plan an evil man is trapped,
But the righteous man sings and rejoices [for his plan brings good things to him].
AMP.7:
The righteous man cares for the rights of the poor,
But the wicked man has no interest in such knowledge.
AMP.8:
Scoffers set a city afire [by stirring up trouble],
But wise men turn away anger [and restore order with their good judgment].
AMP.9:
If a wise man has a controversy with a foolish and arrogant man,
The foolish man [ignores logic and fairness and] only rages or laughs, and there is no peace (rest, agreement).
AMP.10:
The bloodthirsty hate the blameless [because of his integrity],
But the upright are concerned for his life.
AMP.11:
A [shortsighted] fool always loses his temper and displays his anger,
But a wise man [uses self-control and] holds it back.
AMP.12:
If a ruler pays attention to lies [and encourages corruption],
All his officials will become wicked.
AMP.13:
The poor man and the oppressor have this in common:
The LORD gives light to the eyes of both.
AMP.14:
If a king faithfully and truthfully judges the poor,
His throne shall be established forever.
AMP.15:
The rod and reproof (godly instruction) give wisdom,
But a child who gets his own way brings shame to his mother.
AMP.16:
When the wicked are in authority, transgression increases,
But the righteous will see the downfall of the wicked.
AMP.17:
Correct your son, and he will give you comfort;
Yes, he will delight your soul.
AMP.18:
Where there is no vision [no revelation of God and His word], the people are unrestrained;
But happy and blessed is he who keeps the law [of God].
AMP.19:
A servant will not be corrected by words alone;
For though he understands, he will not respond [nor pay attention].
AMP.20:
Do you see a [conceited] man who speaks quickly [offering his opinions or answering without thinking]?
There is more hope for a [thickheaded] fool than for him.
AMP.21:
He who pampers his slave from childhood
Will find him to be a son in the end.
AMP.22:
An angry man stirs up strife,
And a hot-tempered and undisciplined man commits many transgressions.
AMP.23:
A man's pride and sense of self-importance will bring him down,
But he who has a humble spirit will obtain honor.
AMP.24:
Whoever is partner with a thief hates his own life;
He hears the curse [when swearing an oath to testify], but discloses nothing [and commits perjury by omission].
AMP.25:
The fear of man brings a snare,
But whoever trusts in and puts his confidence in the LORD will be exalted and safe.
AMP.26:
Many seek the ruler's favor,
But justice for man comes from the LORD.
AMP.27:
An unjust man is repulsive to the righteous,
And he who is upright in the way [of the LORD] is repulsive to the wicked.

MSG.1:
For people who hate discipline
and only get more stubborn,
There'll come a day when life tumbles in and they break,
but by then it'll be too late to help them.
MSG.2:
When good people run things, everyone is glad,
but when the ruler is bad, everyone groans.
MSG.3:
If you love wisdom, you'll delight your parents,
but you'll destroy their trust if you run with whores.
MSG.4:
A leader of good judgment gives stability;
an exploiting leader leaves a trail of waste.
MSG.5:
A flattering neighbor is up to no good;
he's probably planning to take advantage of you.
MSG.6:
Evil people fall into their own traps;
good people run the other way, glad to escape.
MSG.7:
The good-hearted understand what it's like to be poor;
the hardhearted haven't the faintest idea.
MSG.8:
A gang of cynics can upset a whole city;
a group of sages can calm everyone down.
MSG.9:
A sage trying to work things out with a fool
gets only scorn and sarcasm for his trouble.
MSG.10:
Murderers hate honest people;
moral folks encourage them.
MSG.11:
A fool lets it all hang out;
a sage quietly mulls it over.
MSG.12:
When a leader listens to malicious gossip,
all the workers get infected with evil.
MSG.13:
The poor and their abusers have at least something in common:
they can both see—their sight, God's gift!
MSG.14:
Leadership gains authority and respect
when the voiceless poor are treated fairly.
MSG.15:
Wise discipline imparts wisdom;
spoiled adolescents embarrass their parents.
MSG.16:
When degenerates take charge, crime runs wild,
but the righteous will eventually observe their collapse.
MSG.17:
Discipline your children; you'll be glad you did—
they'll turn out delightful to live with.
MSG.18:
If people can't see what God is doing,
they stumble all over themselves;
But when they attend to what he reveals,
they are most blessed.
MSG.19:
It takes more than talk to keep workers in line;
mere words go in one ear and out the other.
MSG.20:
Observe the people who always talk before they think—
even simpletons are better off than they are.
MSG.21:
If you let people treat you like a doormat,
you'll be quite forgotten in the end.
MSG.22:
Angry people stir up a lot of discord;
the intemperate stir up trouble.
MSG.23:
Pride lands you flat on your face;
humility prepares you for honors.
MSG.24:
Befriend an outlaw
and become an enemy to yourself.
When the victims cry out,
you'll be included in their curses
if you're a coward to their cause in court.
MSG.25:
The fear of human opinion disables;
trusting in God protects you from that.
MSG.26:
Everyone tries to get help from the leader,
but only God will give us justice.
MSG.27:
Good people can't stand the sight of deliberate evil;
the wicked can't stand the sight of well-chosen goodness.

NIV.1:
A man who remains stiff-necked after many rebukes
will suddenly be destroyed—without remedy.
NIV.2:
When the righteous thrive, the people rejoice;
when the wicked rule, the people groan.
NIV.3:
A man who loves wisdom brings joy to his father,
but a companion of prostitutes squanders his wealth.
NIV.4:
By justice a king gives a country stability,
but one who is greedy for bribes tears it down.
NIV.5:
Whoever flatters his neighbor
is spreading a net for his feet.
NIV.6:
An evil man is snared by his own sin,
but a righteous one can sing and be glad.
NIV.7:
The righteous care about justice for the poor,
but the wicked have no such concern.
NIV.8:
Mockers stir up a city,
but wise men turn away anger.
NIV.9:
If a wise man goes to court with a fool,
the fool rages and scoffs, and there is no peace.
NIV.10:
Bloodthirsty men hate a man of integrity
and seek to kill the upright.
NIV.11:
A fool gives full vent to his anger,
but a wise man keeps himself under control.
NIV.12:
If a ruler listens to lies,
all his officials become wicked.
NIV.13:
The poor man and the oppressor have this in common:
The Lord gives sight to the eyes of both.
NIV.14:
If a king judges the poor with fairness,
his throne will always be secure.
NIV.15:
The rod of correction imparts wisdom,
but a child left to himself disgraces his mother.
NIV.16:
When the wicked thrive, so does sin,
but the righteous will see their downfall.
NIV.17:
Discipline your son, and he will give you peace;
he will bring delight to your soul.
NIV.18:
Where there is no revelation, the people cast off restraint;
but blessed is he who keeps the law.
NIV.19:
A servant cannot be corrected by mere words;
though he understands, he will not respond.
NIV.20:
Do you see a man who speaks in haste?
There is more hope for a fool than for him.
NIV.21:
If a man pampers his servant from youth,
he will bring grief in the end.
NIV.22:
An angry man stirs up dissension,
and a hot-tempered one commits many sins.
NIV.23:
A man’s pride brings him low,
but a man of lowly spirit gains honor.
NIV.24:
The accomplice of a thief is his own enemy;
he is put under oath and dare not testify.
NIV.25:
Fear of man will prove to be a snare,
but whoever trusts in the Lord is kept safe.
NIV.26:
Many seek an audience with a ruler,
but it is from the Lord that man gets justice.
NIV.27:
The righteous detest the dishonest;
the wicked detest the upright.

NRSVCE.1:
One who is often reproved, yet remains stubborn,
will suddenly be broken beyond healing.
NRSVCE.2:
When the righteous are in authority, the people rejoice;
but when the wicked rule, the people groan.
NRSVCE.3:
A child who loves wisdom makes a parent glad,
but to keep company with prostitutes is to squander one’s substance.
NRSVCE.4:
By justice a king gives stability to the land,
but one who makes heavy exactions ruins it.
NRSVCE.5:
Whoever flatters a neighbor
is spreading a net for the neighbor’s feet.
NRSVCE.6:
In the transgression of the evil there is a snare,
but the righteous sing and rejoice.
NRSVCE.7:
The righteous know the rights of the poor;
the wicked have no such understanding.
NRSVCE.8:
Scoffers set a city aflame,
but the wise turn away wrath.
NRSVCE.9:
If the wise go to law with fools,
there is ranting and ridicule without relief.
NRSVCE.10:
The bloodthirsty hate the blameless,
and they seek the life of the upright.
NRSVCE.11:
A fool gives full vent to anger,
but the wise quietly holds it back.
NRSVCE.12:
If a ruler listens to falsehood,
all his officials will be wicked.
NRSVCE.13:
The poor and the oppressor have this in common:
the LORD gives light to the eyes of both.
NRSVCE.14:
If a king judges the poor with equity,
his throne will be established forever.
NRSVCE.15:
The rod and reproof give wisdom,
but a mother is disgraced by a neglected child.
NRSVCE.16:
When the wicked are in authority, transgression increases,
but the righteous will look upon their downfall.
NRSVCE.17:
Discipline your children, and they will give you rest;
they will give delight to your heart.
NRSVCE.18:
Where there is no prophecy, the people cast off restraint,
but happy are those who keep the law.
NRSVCE.19:
By mere words servants are not disciplined,
for though they understand, they will not give heed.
NRSVCE.20:
Do you see someone who is hasty in speech?
There is more hope for a fool than for anyone like that.
NRSVCE.21:
A slave pampered from childhood
will come to a bad end.
NRSVCE.22:
One given to anger stirs up strife,
and the hothead causes much transgression.
NRSVCE.23:
A person’s pride will bring humiliation,
but one who is lowly in spirit will obtain honor.
NRSVCE.24:
To be a partner of a thief is to hate one’s own life;
one hears the victim’s curse, but discloses nothing.
NRSVCE.25:
The fear of others lays a snare,
but one who trusts in the LORD is secure.
NRSVCE.26:
Many seek the favor of a ruler,
but it is from the LORD that one gets justice.
NRSVCE.27:
The unjust are an abomination to the righteous,
but the upright are an abomination to the wicked.

NET.1: The one who stiffens his neck after numerous rebukes will suddenly be destroyed without remedy.
NET.2: When the righteous become numerous, the people rejoice; when the wicked rule, the people groan.
NET.3: The man who loves wisdom brings joy to his father, but whoever associates with prostitutes wastes his wealth.
NET.4: A king brings stability to a land by justice, but one who exacts tribute tears it down.
NET.5: The one who flatters his neighbor spreads a net for his steps.
NET.6: In the transgression of an evil person there is a snare, but a righteous person can sing and rejoice.
NET.7: The righteous person cares for the legal rights of the poor; the wicked does not understand such knowledge.
NET.8: Scornful people inflame a city, but those who are wise turn away wrath.
NET.9: If a wise person goes to court with a foolish person, there is no peace whether he is angry or laughs.
NET.10: Bloodthirsty people hate someone with integrity; as for the upright, they seek his life.
NET.11: A fool lets fly with all his temper, but a wise person keeps it back.
NET.12: If a ruler listens to lies, all his ministers will be wicked.
NET.13: The poor person and the oppressor have this in common: the LORD gives light to the eyes of them both.
NET.14: If a king judges the poor in truth, his throne will be established forever.
NET.15: A rod and reproof impart wisdom, but a child who is unrestrained brings shame to his mother.
NET.16: When the wicked increase, transgression increases, but the righteous will see their downfall.
NET.17: Discipline your child, and he will give you rest; he will bring you happiness.
NET.18: When there is no prophetic vision the people cast off restraint, but the one who keeps the law, blessed is he!
NET.19: A servant cannot be corrected by words, for although he understands, there is no answer.
NET.20: Do you see someone who is hasty in his words? There is more hope for a fool than for him.
NET.21: If someone pampers his servant from youth, he will be a weakling in the end.
NET.22: An angry person stirs up dissension, and a wrathful person is abounding in transgression.
NET.23: A person’s pride will bring him low, but one who has a lowly spirit will gain honor.
NET.24: Whoever shares with a thief is his own enemy; he hears the oath to testify, but does not talk.
NET.25: The fear of people becomes a snare, but whoever trusts in the LORD will be set on high.
NET.26: Many people seek the face of a ruler, but it is from the LORD that one receives justice.
NET.27: An unjust person is an abomination to the righteous, and the one who lives an upright life is an abomination to the wicked.
